asset.title: Two Client Live Sync
asset.purpose: README live walkthrough clip. Judge whether the feature is obvious, visually consistent, and credible as evidence.

judge.summary: The walkthrough effectively demonstrates real-time synchronization between two clients and a server-led agent performing spreadsheet reconciliation. The workflow is complete and highly relevant to collaborative financial operations.

featureClarity.evidence: Clearly shows Client A and Client B updating in real-time as messages are sent and agent actions occur.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Captures the entire sequence from initial message, instant sync, agent trigger, to live cell updates.

visualDesign.evidence: Clean dark-mode interface, though the sudden zoom transitions at 00:05 are slightly jarring.

consistency.evidence: Perfectly aligns with NodeRoom's core elements: spreadsheets, chat, and server-led agents.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Shows actual UI interactions, side-by-side client states, and live agent execution traces.

legibility.evidence: Zoomed-out views make small text and numbers in the spreadsheet columns difficult to read.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for collaborative finance and operations teams needing multi-user spreadsheet sync.

productionHonesty.evidence: Accurately depicts live sync latency and agent execution steps, though heavily edited for pacing.

observedEvidence: Server-led agent triggering and cell locking

defects.observed: Sudden zoom-in transition is disorienting and disrupts the visual flow.
defects.fix: Soften the transition with a smoother ease-in-out animation or maintain a consistent crop.

defects.observed: Spreadsheet cell values are too small to read in the default dual-client view.
defects.fix: Increase the default font size of the spreadsheet component or use a slightly tighter side-by-side layout.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Watch NodeRoom's live sync in action: Client A triggers a server-led agent to reconcile Q3 revenue, instantly updating cells and locking states across both clients without page refreshes.
